title: Fab Inspector Extended JSONLogic Rule
description: Schema for JSONLogic expressions used in Fab Inspector, covering 34 native operators, 5 FabInspector.Operators, and 31 Ric.Operators.

literal.description: A literal value passed through unchanged (not a rule object).

rule.description: Any valid JSONLogic expression: a literal or a single-operator rule object.

ruleObject.description: A JSONLogic rule object with exactly one operator key.

==.description: Loose equality. Compares two values with type coercion (e.g. 1 == "1" is true).

===.description: Strict equality. Compares two values without type coercion (e.g. 1 === "1" is false).

!=.description: Loose inequality. True if two values are not loosely equal.

!==.description: Strict inequality. True if two values are not strictly equal.

>.description: Greater than. Compares two values with numeric coercion.

>=.description: Greater than or equal. Compares two values with numeric coercion.

<.description: Less than. With 2 args: a < b. With 3 args (between): a < b < c.

<=.description: Less than or equal. With 2 args: a <= b. With 3 args (between): a <= b <= c.

!.description: Logical NOT. Negates a single value. Accepts bare value or single-element array.

!!.description: Double negation (cast to boolean). Returns true for truthy values, false for falsy.

and.description: Logical AND. Returns the first falsy value or the last value if all are truthy. Short-circuits.

or.description: Logical OR. Returns the first truthy value or the last value if all are falsy. Short-circuits.

if.description: If/then/elseif/then/.../else. Variadic: [cond1, then1, cond2, then2, ..., else]. Supports chained conditions.

in.description: Membership test. Checks if the first arg is contained in the second (string substring or array membership).

cat.description: String concatenation. Joins all arguments into a single string. Accepts bare value or array.

substr.description: Substring extraction. Args: [string, start] or [string, start, length]. Negative indices count from end.

+.description: Addition / numeric cast. Sums all arguments. A single argument is cast to number.

-.description: Subtraction / negation. With 2 args: a - b. With 1 arg: -a (negation).

/.description: Division. Divides the first argument by the second.

%.description: Modulo. Returns the remainder of dividing the first argument by the second.

min.description: Minimum. Returns the smallest value from the arguments.

max.description: Maximum. Returns the largest value from the arguments.

merge.description: Merge arrays. Flattens arguments into a single array. Non-array arguments are wrapped.

var.description: Data access. Retrieves a value from the data object by dot-notation path. Args: path (string/number/null) or [path, default].

missing.description: Returns an array of keys that are missing from the data object.

missing_some.description: Returns missing keys only if fewer than the minimum required are present. Args: [minimum_required, [keys...]].

filter.description: Array filter. Returns elements where the logic evaluates truthy. Args: [dataArray, logicRule]. Use {"var":""} for current element.

map.description: Array map. Transforms each element using the logic rule. Args: [dataArray, logicRule]. Use {"var":""} for current element.

all.description: Array all. True if every element satisfies the logic rule. Returns false for empty arrays.

none.description: Array none. True if no element satisfies the logic rule. Returns true for empty arrays.

some.description: Array some. True if at least one element satisfies the logic rule. Returns false for empty arrays.

reduce.description: Array reduce. Accumulates a value over the array. Args: [dataArray, logicRule, initialValue]. Use {"var":"current"} and {"var":"accumulator"} inside logic.

apiget.description: [FabInspector] HTTP GET against Power BI or Fabric REST APIs. Args: [urlTemplate, ...params]. URL placeholders like {paramName} are substituted by positional parameters.

daxquery.description: [FabInspector] Execute a DAX query against a Fabric Semantic Model. Args: [daxQuery, workspaceId?, semanticModelId?, includeNulls?, impersonatedUserName?].

dfsget.description: [FabInspector] HTTP GET against OneLake DFS endpoints. Args: [dfsUrlTemplate, ...params]. URL must be HTTPS to *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com.

rectoverlap.description: [FabInspector] Detect overlapping rectangles. Args: [rectanglesArray] or [rectanglesArray, margin]. Each rectangle: {name, x, y, width, height}. Returns names of overlapping rectangles.

scannerapi.description: [FabInspector] Invoke the Power BI Admin Scanner API. Args: [workspaceIds, lineage?, datasourceDetails?, datasetSchema?, datasetExpressions?, getArtifactUsers?].

coalesce.description: [Ric] Returns the first non-null value from the arguments. Unlike 'or', only skips null — not other falsy values.

count.description: [Ric] Counts the number of items in an array. Returns an integer.

datediff.description: [Ric] Computes the difference between two ISO 8601 dates. Args: [date1, date2] or [date1, date2, unit]. Unit: seconds|minutes|hours|days (default: days). Result is date2 - date1.

distinct.description: [Ric] Removes duplicate values from an array using deep equality. Returns deduplicated array.

drillvar.description: [Ric] Navigate to a variable using a path string with '>' separator. Args: [path] or [path, defaultValue].

filesize.description: [Ric] Returns the file or folder size in bytes for the given path. Works with local and remote file systems.

filetextsearchcount.description: [Ric] Reads a file and counts regex pattern matches. Args: [filePath, regexPattern]. Returns integer count.

fromyamlfile.description: [Ric] Reads and parses a YAML file, converting it to a JSON object. Args: [filePath].

hasprop.description: [Ric] Checks if a JSON object contains a property with the given key. Args: [object, key]. Returns boolean. Distinguishes null value from missing property.

isnullorempty.description: [Ric] Returns true if the value is null, empty string, 0, or empty array.

keys.description: [Ric] Returns an array of property names from a JSON object.

let.description: [Ric] Variable bindings. Args: [bindingsObject, bodyRule]. Evaluates binding expressions and makes them available via {"var":"name"} in the body. Useful for caching expensive operations.

now.description: [Ric] Returns the current UTC time as an ISO 8601 string. Args: [] or [offset] or [offset, unit]. Unit: seconds|minutes|hours|days|months|years.

part.description: [Ric] Queries part information for a given file/folder path. Args: [partName]. Returns part content from the current inspection context.

partinfo.description: [Ric] Queries advanced part information including file size and type. Args: [partPath].

path.description: [Ric] Evaluates a JSONPath expression (RFC 9535) against the current data. Args: [jsonPath] or [jsonPath, defaultValue]. Returns array of matching values.

query.description: [Ric] Applies a JSONLogic rule using the first argument as contextData. Args: [inputData, logicRule]. Useful for transforming or querying nested data.

regexextract.description: [Ric] Extracts regex matches from a string. Args: [string, pattern] or [string, pattern, captureGroupIndex]. Returns array of matches.

diff.description: [Ric] Set difference. Returns items in the first array that are not in the second. Uses deep equality.

equalsets.description: [Ric] Set equality test. Returns empty array if sets are equal, populated array otherwise. Uses deep equality, order-independent.

intersect.description: [Ric] Set intersection. Returns items present in both arrays. Uses deep equality.

symdiff.description: [Ric] Symmetric difference. Returns items in either array but not both. Uses deep equality.

union.description: [Ric] Set union. Combines both arrays, removing duplicates. Uses deep equality.

slice.description: [Ric] Extracts a sub-array. Args: [array, start] or [array, start, end]. Follows JavaScript Array.prototype.slice() semantics; negative indices count from end.

strcontains.description: [Ric] Counts regex pattern matches in a string. Args: [searchString, regexPattern]. Returns integer count.

strjoin.description: [Ric] Joins array elements into a single string with a separator. Args: [array, separator].

strsplit.description: [Ric] Splits a string by a delimiter. Args: [string, delimiter]. Returns array of parts.

torecord.description: [Ric] Constructs a JSON object from alternating key-value pairs. Args: [key1, value1, key2, value2, ...]. Requires an even number of arguments.

tostring.description: [Ric] Converts a value to its JSON string representation.

typeof.description: [Ric] Returns the JSON type of a value as a string: "string", "number", "boolean", "array", "object", or "null".

values.description: [Ric] Returns an array of property values from a JSON object.

letBindings.description: An object mapping variable names to JSONLogic expressions. Each key becomes available via {"var": "name"} in the body.

args_unary.description: A single argument: bare value or single-element array.

args_binary.description: Exactly two arguments in an array.

args_binary_or_ternary.description: Two or three arguments in an array.

args_unary_or_binary.description: One or two arguments: bare value, or array of 1-2 elements.

args_variadic.description: One or more arguments in an array.

args_bare_or_array.description: A single value or an array of values (for cat, merge, +, *, etc.).
